items.title: Met with His Majesty King Hamad bin Isa Al Khalifa of Bahrain to reaffirm our enduring partnership and our commitment to Bahrain's security. We stand united on regional stability, a free and open Strait of Hormuz, and preventing Iran from acquiring a nuclear weapon. Iran's attacks on Bahrain were unacceptable, and the United States stands with the people and government of Bahrain.

items.title: Oil price falls back to pre-Iran war levels
items.summary: Signs that traffic through the key Strait of Hormuz shipping route is gradually resuming has helped to push the oil price down.

items.title: Stocks Rally as Micron Lifts AI Optimism Ahead of PCE | Bloomberg Brief 6/25/2026
items.summary: US equity futures jump after Micron's forecast shattered expectations. Oil prices slide back to pre-war levels as investors look ahead to PCE numbers. Sanjeev Rana of CLSA breaks down Micron's earnings. Stephanie Guild of Robinhood discusses what strength in memory chips could mean for the broader market. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Major Saudi Oil Terminal to Restart as Gulf Reboot Ramps Up
items.summary: Top oil exporter Saudi Arabia is set to restart loading crude at the giant Ras Tanura terminal inside the Persian Gulf, a landmark moment in the resumption of Middle Eastern supplies after the Iran war.

items.title: Iraq Asks for Higher Oil Output Quota as OPEC Membership Rankles
items.summary: Iraq said it wants OPEC’s permission to pump more crude as the producer group’s limits on what it can sell hinder Baghdad’s scope to recoup oil sales that were lost because of the Iran war.

items.title: ECB Rate-Hike Forecasts Pared Back as Oil Regains Pre-War Level
items.summary: A sharp drop in oil prices amid progress in Middle-East peace talks has prompted some economists to scale back expectations for European Central Bank interest-rate increases.

items.title: AI IPO Wealth Seen Bolstering Luxury Industry Hurt by Iran War
items.summary: The luxury sector is set to grow more slowly than previously predicted because of the Iran war, but wealth creation from the SpaceX listing and future US share sales by OpenAI and Anthropic could cushion the blow for struggling brands selling pricey handbags and watches, according to Bain & Co.

items.summary: After two years of trying to get a foothold in neighboring Croatia by buying Addiko Bank AG, Slovenia’s largest lender isn’t ready to give up.

items.title: Treasuries Climb as Benign US Inflation Eases Bets on a Fed Hike
items.summary: Short-term Treasuries jumped as a benign reading of the Federal Reserve’s favored inflation gauge slightly dragged on traders’ expectations for an interest-rate hike in the months ahead.

items.title: Why Fed Chairman Warsh’s Pivot Risks Confusing Markets
items.summary: Bill Dudley, former New York Fed President and Bloomberg Opinion columnist, says streamlining the Federal Open Market Committee’s policy statement is appropriate, but Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh needs to make changes with greater care than he has shown to date. Dudley says Warsh may risk confusing markets and the central bank itself. His opinions are his own. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Did the Iran war just prove that China’s ageing DF-15Bs are still big guns?
items.summary:
Lessons from the Iran war suggest that China’s ageing Dongfeng-15B short-range missiles may be more useful in penetrating modern missile defences than previously thought, according to military analysts.
The suggestion surfaced this month in an article published by Ordnance Science and Technology which said that missiles with biconic manoeuvring re-entry vehicles (MRVs) could achieve a penetration

items.title: With a Fragile Ceasefire under Threat, What Future for the Strait of Hormuz?
items.summary: The U.S.-Israeli war with Iran prompted Tehran to close the Strait of Hormuz, allowing only selected ships to pass through. In this Q&A, Crisis Group experts Yasmine Farouk, Chris Newton and Ali Vaez consider what could come next in this vital maritime trade corridor.

HK Electric raises fuel charge by nearly 34% and further increases expected
items.summary:
HK Electric has announced a nearly 34 per cent rise in its fuel charge, after a double-digit increase last month, as the war in the Middle East contributed to a “significant rise” in global fuel prices.
The utility, which mainly serves Hong Kong Island and Lamma Island, also warned on Thursday that the charge was expected to continue rising.
